$generated: DO NOT EDIT — run `pnpm gen:tools`. Source: createToolRegistry() + mcp TOOL_DEFINITIONS.

tools.name: add_task_dependency
tools.description: Add a blocking dependency between two tasks.

tools.name: advance_phase
tools.description: Advance a thread to the next phase. Requires autonomy level ≥ 2.

tools.name: agent.dispatch
tools.description: Dispatch another AoA agent by inserting a wakeup row. Does NOT call heartbeat directly — the dispatcher's drain loop picks it up. Respects hop-count cap (max 3) and dedupes within a single thread context.

tools.name: analyze_workload
tools.description: Get workload balance insights from the suggestion engine.

tools.name: approval_decision
tools.description: Decide an approval: approve, reject, request revision, or resubmit. Founder-only, and always confirmed before it runs (irreversible governance action).

tools.name: archive_stale_memory
tools.description: Archive approved memory items whose accessedAt is older than the threshold (default 90 days). Founder approval required.

tools.name: assign_task
tools.description: Assign a task to an agent or human. Assignee means who does the task; responsible human ownership is separate.

tools.name: attach_task_artifact
tools.description: Attach your work product to a task as a deliverable: creates a new artifact (document, code, report, etc.) authored by you, links it to the task, and records it in the task's output index. Use this to hand back the result of your work.

tools.name: attach_to_thread
tools.description: Move a thread_inbox_items entry to a real thread as a new discussion entry.

tools.name: create_agent
tools.description: Create a new worker agent. Founder-only.

tools.name: create_artifact
tools.description: Create a new artifact (document, code, report, etc.) and optionally link it to a thread entry.

tools.name: create_artifact_version
tools.description: Create a new version of an existing artifact (Engineer iteration loop).

tools.name: create_department
tools.description: Create a new department. Founder-only.

tools.name: create_goal
tools.description: Create a new goal with optional department scope.

tools.name: create_task
tools.description: Create a new task with title, optional description, priority, department, goal, assignee, and responsible human. Assignee means who does the task; responsible human owns the outcome.

tools.name: create_workflow_template
tools.description: Create a reusable workflow template with steps and dependencies.

tools.name: defer_inbox_to_human
tools.description: Finalize an inbound item you are UNSURE about: leave it in the Inbox for the founder to triage. Call this instead of returning silently when no thread is a confident home and a new thread isn't clearly warranted.

tools.name: delegate_to_subagent
tools.description: Delegate a task to a named AoA sub-agent by enqueuing a wakeup request. The sub-agent will be triggered asynchronously by the AoA dispatcher.

tools.name: detect_conflicts
tools.description: Check if proposed memory content conflicts with existing items.

tools.name: extract_decisions
tools.description: Extract decision-type memory candidates from a thread (filtered subset of extract_memory_candidates).

tools.name: extract_from_content
tools.description: Trigger extraction of tasks and memory items from a discussion entry.

tools.name: extract_insights
tools.description: Extract insight-type memory candidates from a thread (filtered subset of extract_memory_candidates).

tools.name: extract_memory_candidates
tools.description: Run LLM extraction over thread entries and return structured memory candidates (decisions, insights, references, etc.). Does not persist — pair with submit_extracted_items or propose_memory_from_thread to act on the results.

tools.name: extract_references
tools.description: Extract reference-type memory candidates from a thread (filtered subset of extract_memory_candidates).

tools.name: find_humans
tools.description: Find company humans by profile, role, responsibility, and capability documents. Read-only; use before routing or escalating work.

tools.name: find_similar_memory
tools.description: Find semantically similar memory items using vector search.

tools.name: find_similar_memory_hnsw
tools.description: Find existing memory items semantically similar to a query via direct HNSW cosine search.

tools.name: find_similar_threads
tools.description: Find threads similar to a text query via embedding cosine similarity (HNSW).

tools.name: forget_working_context
tools.description: Archive temporary scoped Commander working context.

tools.name: get_approval
tools.description: Read one approval by id: its type, status, payload, and requester. Use to inspect a specific pending decision before acting on it.

tools.name: get_approval_tasks
tools.description: List the tasks an approval is gating (what unblocks if it is approved).

tools.name: get_heartbeat_context
tools.description: Read a task plus its 10 most recent comments — including the auto-generated run-summary an agent posts after each run (outcome, duration, cost, files touched). Use to see what an agent actually did on a task.

tools.name: get_task
tools.description: Read the full context of a single task (your assigned task): identifier, title, description, status, priority, source discussion thread, assignee, responsible human owner, and execution workspace. Assignee means who does the task; responsible human owns the outcome.

tools.name: get_thread_summary
tools.description: Return a thread's summary, intent, phase, and crew participants.

tools.name: hub.readCurationContext
tools.description: Read bounded, redacted hub item context for Steward curation. This returns hub envelopes and source pointers only; it never reads raw source bodies or mutates lifecycle state.

tools.name: hub.updateCurationSummary
tools.description: Write a bounded Steward curation summary or explanation for a hub item. This only updates display curation metadata and never resolves, archives, approves, rejects, or changes source-side state.

tools.name: instantiate_workflow
tools.description: Create tasks from a workflow template.

tools.name: link_discussion_to_project
tools.description: Scope a discussion to a project, department, or goal.

tools.name: list_approval_comments
tools.description: List the discussion/comments on an approval (context for the decision).

tools.name: list_approvals
tools.description: List the company's approval requests (governance decisions awaiting a call), newest first. Optional filters: status (pending|approved|rejected|revision_requested|…), type. Use when asked what needs approval / what is waiting on the founder.

tools.name: list_thread_cards
tools.description: Fetch routing cards (summaryText + routingTerms) for active threads. Use this to get candidate threads before deciding where an inbound item belongs.

tools.name: notify_owner
tools.description: Send a notification to the thread owner. Use when the crew needs human attention or approval.

tools.name: post_entry
tools.description: Post a message to a thread as this agent. Use for crew coordination, summaries, and @mentions.

tools.name: post_task_comment
tools.description: Post a comment back onto a task (typically your assigned task) — for example a progress note, a summary of what you did, or a question for the founder. The comment is recorded as authored by you.

tools.name: promote_inbox_to_thread
tools.description: Create a new thread from an inbound inbox item. At full_auto dial: auto-creates the thread. At auto_attach/suggest dial: records a 'suggest_new' decision surfaced to the founder.

tools.name: propose_crew_work
tools.description: Propose crew work for this thread. Resolves the effective autonomy (thread or company level) and routes through the unified D11 chokepoint. At autonomy L1 the scope card is written and awaits human approval; at L2 the card is written and immediately auto-approved, dispatching the assigned agents.

tools.name: propose_memory_from_thread
tools.description: Propose a memory item (status='pending', founder approves) seeded from a thread. Inherits visibility + scope from the source thread. Private threads may only propose working or active_context layers.

tools.name: query_activity
tools.description: Get recent activity log entries.

tools.name: query_agents
tools.description: List agents with optional department filter.

tools.name: query_artifacts
tools.description: List artifacts linked to a thread via discussion_entry_attachments.

tools.name: query_budget
tools.description: Get budget summary for the company.

tools.name: query_company
tools.description: Get the current company's identity: name, vision, mission, issue prefix, and stage. Call this whenever you need to know who you are working for.

tools.name: query_company_artifacts
tools.description: List the company's recent artifacts (deliverables) across all threads and tasks, newest first. Optional filters: type (document|presentation|code|design|report|other), status (draft|active|archived), limit (default 20, max 50). Use when asked what artifacts/documents/deliverables exist.

tools.name: query_departments
tools.description: List all departments.

tools.name: query_dependency_chain
tools.description: Analyze task dependency chain — find what blocks a task or what it blocks.

tools.name: query_extracted_items
tools.description: List extracted items from a thread (decisions, tasks, insights, references, etc).

tools.name: query_goals
tools.description: List goals with optional status filter.

tools.name: query_human_context
tools.description: Read a company human's operational context bundle by user id, or resolve a natural-language query to the right human when there is exactly one match. Intended for Commander orchestration.

tools.name: query_humans
tools.description: List company humans from the team roster. Use for broad questions like who is in this org, who is on the team, or which humans exist.

tools.name: query_memory
tools.description: Search memory items with optional layer and text filters.

tools.name: query_tasks
tools.description: List and filter tasks. Returns tasks matching the given filters.

tools.name: query_team_roster
tools.description: Read the unified company team roster and hierarchy across humans and org agents. Use for broad questions like who is on the team, who reports to whom, org chart, or humans and agents together.

tools.name: query_threads
tools.description: List threads (discussions) in this company. Optionally filter by phase or scope.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read an artifact's content. Returns the latest version by default, or a specific version number.

tools.name: remember_working_context
tools.description: Remember temporary scoped Commander working context for the current project, goal, task, or conversation.

tools.name: request_thread_workspace
tools.description: Create an execution_workspace scoped to a thread (Engineer-only; opt-in for interactive/dev-server work).

tools.name: search_discussions
tools.description: Search discussions by title.

tools.name: set_task_status
tools.description: Move your own assigned task forward to a new status (for example from in_progress to in_review, or to done). Whether a transition is allowed depends on your autonomy level — moving a task to review requires at least Assist, and completing it requires Drive. Use this when you have finished a unit of work and want to hand the task to the next stage.

tools.name: spin_off_thread
tools.description: Create a new thread spun off from an existing thread. Optionally copy seed entries; writes a thread_links row of kind 'spinoff'.

tools.name: submit_extracted_items
tools.description: Persist structured extracted items (decisions, tasks, insights, etc.) for a discussion entry and mark the entry's extraction as completed. Called by the discussion-extraction agent to write its results.

tools.name: suggest_improvements
tools.description: Run all detectors and return pending improvement suggestions.

tools.name: suggest_memory
tools.description: Propose a new memory item for founder approval (status: pending).

tools.name: thread.createLink
tools.description: Create a typed link between two threads.

tools.name: thread.listEntries
tools.description: Read entries from a thread in chronological order (capped at 200).

tools.name: thread.postScopeProposal
tools.description: Post a scope_proposal entry to a thread and populate thread_plan_steps (single transaction).

tools.name: thread.setIntent
tools.description: Set the intent classification on a thread (e.g. planning, research, problem, alignment).

tools.name: thread.updateSummary
tools.description: Update a thread's summary text + routing terms, then queue embedding regeneration.

tools.name: update_agent
tools.description: Update agent configuration. Founder-only.

tools.name: update_company_identity
tools.description: Update the company's vision and/or mission statement. Only call this after the user has reviewed and approved the new text. Gated to founders only.

tools.name: update_memory
tools.description: Update an existing memory item's content or layer.

tools.name: update_task
tools.description: Update an existing task's title, status, priority, or responsible human. Use assign_task for assignee reassignment.

tools.name: update_working_context
tools.description: Update temporary scoped Commander working context.

tools.name: use_skill
tools.description: Load a skill's full instructions into context. Call this before applying any skill. The skill content will be available for the rest of this conversation.

tools.name: wakeup_agent
tools.description: Trigger an agent's heartbeat to start working.

tools.name: write_memory
tools.description: Create a memory item (status='pending', founder approves per Critical Rule #6) and enqueue it for RAG indexing. Use for capturing important knowledge discovered during task execution that should be reviewed and promoted to company memory. For temporary working notes scoped only to this agent, prefer the MCP memory.retain tool with scopeToSelf=true and layer=working.

tools.name: add-approval-comment
tools.description: Add a comment to an approval. Any role may comment on approvals they can see.

tools.name: add-task-comment
tools.description: Add a comment to a task the caller can access

tools.name: approval-decision
tools.description: Approve, reject, request revision, or resubmit an approval. Founders + team leads only. Team leads limited to approvals with at least one task in their scope.

tools.name: ask_founder
tools.description: Ask the founder a question and block (up to ~5 min) for the answer. For org/heartbeat task-execution agents during an active run only. Surfaces in the Inbox as a question the founder answers (free-text, or one of your options). On timeout the run is parked and you get {answered:false, status:"parked"} — stop gracefully; do not retry.

tools.name: attach-artifact-version
tools.description: Add a new immutable version to an artifact

tools.name: create-approval
tools.description: Create a new approval request. Founders + team leads only. Team leads must link at least one task from their scope.

tools.name: create-task
tools.description: Create a task directly in the caller's company (RBAC scoped). Does NOT route through Discussion; use debrief-push for unstructured content extraction (Decision #14 revised)

tools.name: debrief-push
tools.description: Push content into the Debrief pipeline

tools.name: get-agent
tools.description: Get a single agent by id (RBAC scoped)

tools.name: get-approval
tools.description: Get an approval by id (RBAC scoped; cross-company 404)

tools.name: get-approval-tasks
tools.description: List tasks linked to an approval. Scoped users see only tasks in their projects.

tools.name: get-heartbeat-context
tools.description: Return a compact { task, recentComments } payload for a task (last 10 comments)

tools.name: get-project
tools.description: Get a single project by id (RBAC scoped)

tools.name: get-task-comment
tools.description: Get a single task comment by id (RBAC scoped via its task)

tools.name: get-task-document
tools.description: Return the task's document artifact with its latest version (content + metadata)

tools.name: link-task-approval
tools.description: Link an existing approval to an existing task. Founders + team leads only.

tools.name: list-agents
tools.description: List agents in the caller's company, scoped by RBAC

tools.name: list-approval-comments
tools.description: List comments on an approval (RBAC scoped)

tools.name: list-approvals
tools.description: List approvals in the caller's company. Scoped users see only approvals linked to tasks in their projects. Filterable by status and type.

tools.name: list-projects
tools.description: List projects (departments + projects) in the caller's company

tools.name: list-task-approvals
tools.description: List approvals linked to a task (RBAC scoped via the task)

tools.name: list-task-comments
tools.description: List comments on a task (RBAC scoped)

tools.name: list-task-document-revisions
tools.description: List all immutable revisions of the task's document artifact, ordered ascending by version number

tools.name: list-task-documents
tools.description: List document artifacts attached to a task (0 or 1 — AoA has 1:1 task↔artifact)

tools.name: list-tasks
tools.description: List tasks in the caller's company with RBAC scoping. Supports filters: status, projectId, assigneeAgentId, assigneeUserId, responsibleUserId, touchedByUserId, unreadForUserId, labelId, q

tools.name: me
tools.description: Return the authenticated caller's identity and role

tools.name: memory.get
tools.description: Fetch a single approved memory item by id. Returns 404 when the item is outside the caller's RBAC scope.

tools.name: memory.retain
tools.description: Persist an observation to memory. When called by an agent actor with scopeToSelf=true AND layer="working", the item is auto-approved into that agent's personal working-memory bucket. Self-scoped retains targeting identity/domain/active_context — and all non-agent writes — instead create a pending item awaiting founder review (Critical Rule #6: only the founder approves identity/domain).

tools.name: memory.search
tools.description: Search company memory using multi-pathway retrieval (semantic + keyword + temporal). Returns top-K items ranked by RRF + trust weighting, scoped to the caller's RBAC visibility.

tools.name: memory.write
tools.description: Create a memory item and immediately enqueue it for RAG embedding. Always creates with status='pending' — the founder must approve before the item enters the Knowledge Base (Critical Rule #6: agents/MCP cannot self-approve identity or domain memory). Use this when you have structured knowledge to persist; use debrief-push for unstructured content that needs LLM extraction first.

tools.name: restore-task-document-revision
tools.description: Create a NEW document artifact version whose content is copied from the specified older revision. The older revision is never mutated (preserves Decisions #43/#45 — artifact versions are immutable).

tools.name: suggest-memory
tools.description: Create a pending memory suggestion

tools.name: unlink-task-approval
tools.description: Unlink an approval from a task. Founders + team leads only.

tools.name: update-task
tools.description: Update a task's fields (title, description, status, priority, assignee, etc.) with RBAC checks

tools.name: update-task-status
tools.description: Update a task status with permission checks

tools.name: upsert-task-document
tools.description: Create or update the task's document (markdown). If the task already has a document artifact, appends a new immutable version; otherwise creates an artifact of type 'document' and links it to the task. Maps Paperclip's upsert-issue-document to AoA's artifact subsystem.

tools.name: use_skill
tools.description: Load the full instructions for an AoA skill by key (e.g. 'skill:aoa/brainstorm'). Returns the skill's markdown so your model can follow it. Call query_skills first if you are unsure of the available skill keys.
